title: The End of Evangelion Full Extended Edition
display_title: The End of Evangelion Full Extended Edition
wikitext:
[]: The End of Evangelion (Full Extended Edition)             Quote Post by K40s » Fri Oct 25, 2013 5:38 pm I found this on youtube: [LINK REMOVED] [RULES] I'll just leave the original description talk for itself: A version of EoE that a member of the 'Revolution of Evangelion' website made. The user added scenes from Episode 25 and 26 to the EoE movie when they take place in that movie (since Ep25 and 26 takes place during the events EoE). This version is almost like a directors cut of EoE and is way more intense than the original. The REva user and i do not own the Eva series (not shit), Also as I said before i did not make this edited version of EoE and i am only uploading it because i enjoyed it so much i wanted to share it with other Eva fans. I am maybe one of like 20 ppl to have this version of the movie and since there is no way to get it otherwise since the download link is no longer available and has not been for a few years. Anyway, enjoy this masterpiece! It really is amazing!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 25, 2013 8:01 pm Is this the same thing as the concurrency project?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Oct 25, 2013 8:49 pm if it were the FULL extended addition, wouldn't it also have to include the 10 minute live action sequence?

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Sun Oct 27, 2013 12:39 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Is this the same thing as the concurrency project?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 27, 2013 1:55 am What I meant was the alternate live action sequence that was cut from eoe where we discover, amongst other things, that in a world without Shinji, it is strongly implied that Asuka receives Toji's load in her mouth.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 27, 2013 2:05 am I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about, Nacks. What I do want to know is where the hell this can be found, so that I can watch it and then curl up into a little ball and cry, because what you're describing sounds horrific.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Oct 27, 2013 2:20 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about, Nacks. What I do want to know is where the hell this can be found, so that I can watch it and then curl up into a little ball and cry, because what you're describing sounds horrific.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 27, 2013 2:29 am Yeah the video is very easy to find if you google for it and it's also been subbed in English with color coding for the different characters. One thing I've always wondered is if it's anno who provides Shinji's voice at the end of that clip.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 27, 2013 11:28 am ^ Well, you'll know as soon as you can find a clip of the new Miyazaki film. He voices the main, remember? Also: I have no idea what this script is, but I'm irked that it never made it into the movie. *shivers* Yeah, that was creepy as all hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 27, 2013 12:14 pm Anno voices the Black Space God from Mars on the " Neon Genesis Evangelion - After the End Drama CD ", picsubs for which can be found as readily as the " End of Evangelion - Live Action Deleted Scene " fansubs.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Unit XX » Sun Oct 27, 2013 12:16 pm That was awesome! This explained a few things for me. Like, why Shinji decided to abort Instrumentality. Now I'm wondering why Anno and crew decided to only include parts of it in the final film...

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Oct 27, 2013 2:12 pm I thought the Live Action part of EoE was pretty old news.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Mon Oct 28, 2013 1:08 am I really wish I had seen this before the link was removed.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:05 am I wanted to see it too. :( But I guess it wasn't worth the risk of a lawsuit.

[]:             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Mon Oct 28, 2013 5:14 am I got to check some of it before the DMCA hammer fell. It was the English dub, it put the first part of 25 at the start (the Shinji bits) then it continued with the start of 25'. Then the Rei bits (when the creepy Rei face appears) were inserted between the Seele talk with Gendo and Rei at her room. Then most of the rest of EoTV was put after Third Impact, there was no Asuka related EOTV material during the "I don't want to die" lake scene, and all the EoTV scenes ended right before GNR's neck starts spewing blood. I might be wrong because I was skipping through it quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Oct 28, 2013 2:50 pm Eva Unit XX wrote: That was awesome! This explained a few things for me. Like, why Shinji decided to abort Instrumentality. Now I'm wondering why Anno and crew decided to only include parts of it in the final film...

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Oct 31, 2013 11:12 pm Heaven Piercing Man wrote: I got to check some of it before the DMCA hammer fell. It was the English dub, it put the first part of 25 at the start (the Shinji bits) then it continued with the start of 25'. Then the Rei bits (when the creepy Rei face appears) were inserted between the Seele talk with Gendo and Rei at her room. Then most of the rest of EoTV was put after Third Impact, there was no Asuka related EOTV material during the "I don't want to die" lake scene, and all the EoTV scenes ended right before GNR's neck starts spewing blood. I might be wrong because I was skipping through it quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 31, 2013 11:19 pm This sounds like a variation of concurrency project releases. (Which various people have worked on and/or made their own edits throughout the years, it's not just a one person thing. It's just that a version by folks from EGF hasn't been properly released yet.) See this thread if that discussion interests you. I remember seeing at least one or two concurrency video edits on Youtube years ago; both had scenes in slightly different places than you'd expect. Pretty sure they were the dub, too.

[]:             Quote Post by KaiserSeph » Sat Nov 16, 2013 12:24 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: This sounds like a variation of concurrency project releases. (Which various people have worked on and/or made their own edits throughout the years, it's not just a one person thing. It's just that a version by folks from EGF hasn't been properly released yet.) See this thread if that discussion interests you. I remember seeing at least one or two concurrency video edits on Youtube years ago; both had scenes in slightly different places than you'd expect. Pretty sure they were the dub, too.
